name: Council Bluffs Campus
water_source_detail: Council Bluffs Water Works (Missouri R. basin)
notes: Largest metered DC water user in dataset. 1.0B gal evap + 400M blowdown. Peak 2.7M gal/day summer 2024.

name: The Dalles Campus
water_source_detail: City of The Dalles (Hood River / Mt. Hood watershed)
notes: 316% growth since 2012. Now 1/3 of city water for pop. ~16,200. Highest AF/ac in dataset.

name: Columbia DC — Quincy
notes: QWRU saves 380M gal/yr. Near-zero WUE newest bldgs. 97% reduction in potable use post-2021.

name: West Des Moines (5 campuses)
water_source_detail: WDM Water Works (Des Moines + Raccoon Rivers)
notes: City #1 water user 2024. GPT-4 training. Peak July 2022: 11.5M gal/mo.

name: Altoona Campus
notes: 5M+ sq ft. LLaMA AI training. 1.0M MWh/yr renewable.

name: Citadel Campus (Tahoe Reno)
notes: Air-cooled by design. Near-zero water. Switch exited NV Energy grid.

name: WA1 Campus (Quincy)
notes: WUE≈0 design. Closed-loop. 89 MW across 3 buildings (WA11-13).

name: Boulder Campus DC
notes: IBM oldest DC (1967 plant). LEED Silver 2008. Free cooling 63%/yr. 300k sq ft raised floor.

name: Project Blue (planned)
notes: Pre-operational. High water-stress region. At CB intensity would withdraw ~2,400 AF/yr.

name: Ashburn Campus (DC1-15)
notes: World's largest DC hub (Data Center Alley). Virginia DCs consume 25%+ of state electricity.

name: Aurora-Denver Campus
notes: Will become Xcel CO's largest industrial customer.

name: Atlanta Campus (55 Allen)
notes: Southeast colocation hub.

name: Council Bluffs Colo
notes: Adjacent to Google campus.

name: Dublin Campus
notes: Part of Dublin cluster consuming ~80% city electricity.

name: Dublin Grange Castle
notes: Azure EU West. Ireland power grid near capacity.

name: Frankfurt Campus (FR1-8)
notes: Europe's largest DC hub. 42% of Frankfurt city electricity.

name: Amsterdam AMS1-9
notes: Netherlands proposed DC moratorium 2022. Canal heat exchange pilots.

name: Hamina Finland Campus
notes: Former paper mill. Seawater cooling = near-zero freshwater. District heating reuse pilot.

name: Gavle Data Center
notes: Near-year-round air cooling. 100% renewable (Nordic hydro+wind).

name: Singapore Campus (Jurong)
notes: Singapore imposed DC moratorium 2019-2021. Strict WUE limits (<0.2 m³/MWh) on resumption.

name: Singapore DC Campus
notes: Azure Southeast Asia. Hot/humid = challenging for air cooling.

name: Hangzhou Data Center
notes: Alibaba Cloud East China hub. PUE<1.3 mandated by government since 2022.

name: Tokyo DC Campus (Shinjuku)
notes: Ultra-dense urban. Very small land footprint per MW. High seismic risk design requirements.

name: UAE DC Campus (du partnership)
notes: Extreme heat = high cooling load. Desalinated supply = high embodied energy in water.

name: Cerrillos Campus (Santiago)
notes: First Google DC in South America. Water controversy during Chile drought.

name: Sao Paulo Region
notes: AWS SA-East-1. São Paulo region faces periodic severe drought.

name: New York Campus (NY1-11)
notes: Dense urban colocation. Excellent fiber peering (NYIIX). Very small land footprint.

name: CoreSite Denver Campus
notes: Urban enterprise colocation. Moderate scale.

name: Edge Node (generic US urban)
notes: Representative edge/CDN node. Near-zero water. Often in carrier hotels.

name: London Campus (Sovereign House)
notes: Dense urban London. UK grid rapidly decarbonizing.

name: Yondr Dublin Campus
notes: Smaller hyperscale campus. Adiabatic reduces water vs. evap towers.

name: Loudoun County Campus (Reston)
notes: Northern Virginia cluster. Data Center Alley.
